behaviors.description: The API request always includes the full taxonomy label list.
behaviors.justification: A pure unit test of prompt-builder logic can miss wiring/serialization regressions between classifier module and API client. An integration test that exercises real module-to-client request construction is the minimum boundary likely to catch real failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run classifier end-to-end with a test double transport that captures outgoing API payload. Assert captured request contains every configured taxonomy label (no missing/renamed labels) in the field/prompt actually sent.

behaviors.description: Out-of-taxonomy API labels are mapped to "unknown".
behaviors.justification: Failure risk is at the boundary of response parsing + taxonomy validation + return mapping. A unit test on only the mapper could miss parser/client integration issues that leak invalid labels.
behaviors.test_description: Use module with stubbed API response path returning an invalid label (e.g., "medical"). Assert public classifier result is exactly "unknown" and never the raw invalid value.

behaviors.description: Same document yields same classification across repeated calls.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real model stochasticity and provider-side behavior. Mocked/deterministic fakes can always pass and hide true instability. Minimum real failure boundary is repeated calls to the actual AI model.
behaviors.test_description: Call classifier on the same fixed document N times (e.g., 30-100) against the real AI API with production settings. Assert labels are stable (or within explicit allowed variance if policy defines one). Record distribution to detect nondeterministic drift.

behaviors.description: Typical-document token use stays within per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Real failure often comes from mismatch between local token estimation and provider-counted usage after full serialization. Mock usage counters can hide overruns. This needs real API usage accounting.
behaviors.test_description: Send representative documents through classifier to real API and read provider-reported token usage for each call. Assert each call’s total tokens is <= configured budget; fail on any over-budget call.

behaviors.description: On rate-limit responses, module waits and retries instead of surfacing immediate error.
behaviors.justification: Retry correctness depends on real HTTP/error semantics (status, headers like Retry-After, timing/backoff). In-process mocks can hide transport and timing bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Run against a controllable HTTP test endpoint that first returns rate-limit response (429 with/without Retry-After) then success. Assert classifier eventually returns success, made multiple attempts, and respected minimum wait/backoff timing.
